texto: People who work out are so gullible. They think they'll live longer. Well, good luck to them. It's a shame most of them aren't bright enough to realise that the extra time added to their life when they're eighty and too old to do anything productive with it is roughly equal to all the time wasted in the gym when they're young and capable of having fun.
explicacao: Citação de Toni Jordan sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “People who work out are so gullible. They think they'll live longer. Well, good luck to them. It'…” articula reflexao, fitness, health em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como academia e trabalho.

frase_original: People who work out are so gullible. They think they'll live longer. Well, good luck to them. It's a shame most of them aren't bright enough to realise that the extra time added to their life when they're eighty and too old to do anything productive with it is roughly equal to all the time wasted in the gym when they're young and capable of having fun.

texto: You must know this. People disappear. They just go puff. Thin air. Every time you see someone, you never know if you're seeing them for the last time. Drink them in, Alec. Kiss them. It's very important. Never let anyone say goodbye, even for a little while, without kissing them. Press your lips against the people you love. Hands, they can touch anything. Open doors, hold cameras, hang clothes on the line. It's lips that matter.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Toni Jordan sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “You must know this. People disappear. They just go puff. Thin air. Every time you see someone, yo…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, family, love, útil em momentos de termino e love.
